findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[expose\] public section" code/TauCeti/AlgebraicGeometry/WeilDivisor/BasepointChange.lean` hits line 34, while lines 56-63, 93-101, and 137-142 provide public characterization lemmas.
findings.fix: Use a plain `public section` here, or put `@[expose]` only on a declaration whose body a downstream target demonstrably must unfold; otherwise add any missing characterization lemma instead.
findings.issue: The new file puts all declarations in an `@[expose] public section`, exposing bodies such as `weightedBasepointChangeClass` and `unweightedBasepointChangeClass` even though the API already provides coercion and change-base lemmas for consumers.

findings.evidence: Line 522 defines `weightedPointBaseDifference_eq_pointDifference_of_weight_eq_one`; the analogous constant-weight normal form at lines 515-518 is already `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to this lemma so downstream basepoint-change proofs with a weight-one point can normalize without unfolding `weightedPointBaseDifference`.
findings.issue: `weightedPointBaseDifference_eq_pointDifference_of_weight_eq_one` is the normal-form specialization from weighted point-base divisors to `pointDifference` under `w x = 1`, but it is not tagged for `simp`.

summary: The basepoint-change API exposes more implementation than the stated downstream use needs, and one normal-form lemma is missing the expected automation annotation.
